mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes that utilizes Juju for deployment and management, providing a flexible and scalable solution for running Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly suited for users who prefer a model-driven approach to managing Kubernetes resources. Key trade-offs include the need to understand Juju and its operational model, which may introduce complexity for users unfamiliar with it.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
Juju is required to deploy Charmed Kubernetes. This step installs Juju on your local machine.

```bash

sudo snap install juju --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of Juju.

steps.title: Bootstrap Juju Controller
steps.description:
This command initializes a Juju controller, which is necessary for managing your Kubernetes deployment.

```bash

juju bootstrap localhost

```

> Bootstraps a Juju controller on your local machine.

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Charmed Kubernetes bundle, which includes the necessary components for a Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes

```

> Deploys the latest version of Charmed Kubernetes.

steps.title: Verify Deployment
steps.description:
After deployment, this command checks the status of the Kubernetes cluster to ensure all components are running.

```bash

juju status

```

> Displays the status of the deployed services.

steps.title: Post-Installation Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures additional features such as monitoring and autoscaling for your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

juju add-relation kubernetes-master:metrics prometheus:metrics

juju config kubernetes-worker enable-autoscaling=true

```

> Sets up metrics collection.
> Enables autoscaling on worker nodes.

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Charmed Kubernetes deployment and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

juju destroy-controller localhost --destroy-all-models

```

> Destroys the Juju controller and all associated models.

uninstall.title: Cleanup Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command cleans up any orphaned resources that may remain after the uninstallation.

```bash

juju remove-application charmed-kubernetes

```

> Removes the application from Juju.

upgrade.title: Backup and Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your current configuration and data. The upgrade command will update the Charmed Kubernetes deployment to the latest version.

```bash

juju config charmed-kubernetes backup=true

juju upgrade-charm charmed-kubernetes

juju status

```

> Enables backup before the upgrade.
> Upgrades the Charmed Kubernetes charm to the latest version.
> Verifies that the upgrade was successful.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju bootstrap failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Juju controller cannot connect to the specified cloud provider.

**Fix:**
Check your cloud credentials and ensure the cloud is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'charmed-kubernetes not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Charmed Kubernetes bundle is not available in the Juju model.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have added the correct charm repository using 'juju add-remote'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju status shows units in error'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if one or more components of the Kubernetes cluster are not starting correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju debug-log' to get detailed logs and troubleshoot the specific unit.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the Kubernetes API'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Kubernetes API server is down or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Kubernetes control plane and ensure it is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31, improved Juju integration for better resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to run 'juju upgrade-charm' for all deployed charms.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'juju status' showing all units in the 'active' state, indicating that your Charmed Kubernetes cluster is running successfully.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Juju installed and access to a cloud provider or local environment for deployment.
